steps.title: Set the equations equal to find intersection points
steps.content:
When two graphs intersect, they meet at a point where both equations are true simultaneously — that is, they have the same $y$-value for the same $x$-value. So we set the right-hand sides equal:
$$2x + 10 = 2x - 1$$

steps.title: Simplify and identify the contradiction
steps.content:
Subtracting $2x$ from both sides:
$$10 = -1$$
This is false, so there is no $x$ value that makes both equations true at the same time. The graphs do not intersect, so choice A is correct.
